title: dryopteris aculeatum subsp. lobata
intro: Dryopteris aculeatum subsp. lobata, also known as the prickly wood fern, is a subspecies of the Dryopteris aculeatum species, a type of fern native to Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of Dryopteris aculeatum subsp. lobata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and protect against infections, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the prickly wood fern can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues and promoting overall well-being.
